channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/? — 151 replies — 604.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

Open thread • Replies: 151 • Updated: 2026-07-08 15:17 UTC

OP

Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

I joined the military and I wanna know how to get a million dollars. I put 10% of my income into my retirement account and the rest of my free cash into index funds.

Early replies (first 3)

62447635
>>62447595
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sZZaOR4UCnE

62447641
>>62447595
The only smart play is to aim for a 100% disability rating which you can get in ~1 to 4 years (1 enlistment).
I know several guys getting $4000+ a month for life.
I am a vet and I get nothing.

62447664
>>62447595
If you're not high rank or close to information in some other way then it's just another job. Not gonna change much in terms of how getting a million would look like, you'll have to gamble like the rest of us.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62456648
>>62456641
Except Robert is a dumb conservative racist chud, and I'm an anti-imperialist communist. Robert tried and failed to become an Epstein Warrior, whereas you succeeded in becoming one. I think you're both pathetic.

62456675
>>62456648
>Robert is a dumb conservative racist chud
>I'm an anti-imperialist communist
No, actually. Not too long ago you were rooting for "National Syndicalism" so you could argue with more people. You only started being a commie recently because no one else actually gives a shit about Syndicalism.
You are Robert, just some years of constant mindbrokenness and contrarianism apart.

62456736
>>62456675
I flirted with the idea of Fascism for a while, but even then I was looking at it from the left-fash tradition of guys like Edmondo Rossoni. Luckily, after doing more reading on theory and history, I realized that I was right when I was younger and Marxism is the only way forward. There's a reason the Epstein class has worked so tirelessly to discredit and defeat communism, and that's because it's the anti-Epstein ideology.

62456761
>>62456736
Yeah sure buddy, you're doing so much damage to the US by doordashing to single moms in Peoria, Illinois.

62456771
>>62456761
I don't have to do anything to damage America; it is collapsing in front of our eyes under the weight of its own contradictions. Zion Don is the purest essence of America, our spiritual identity distilled into a single man, and now the whole world sees what we really are beyond all the propaganda and lies. We are the Epstein Nation.

62456794
>>62456771
Ok you think pedos run the world, but how does this help OP who is a Jr. Officer in the military invest his money?
Do you see how you've basically hijacked this thread with SNCA?

It's the opposite of advice, you are a cognitohazard.

62456800
>>62456794
Everyone knows the ruling class that controls the economy and government are bunch of pedophiles. They're criminals, and they indulge in whatever crimes they can get away with. This is an obvious fact of life, and always has been.

62456828
>>62456800
Wrong answer. Let's try again!
What is the best financial move for OP who is a Jr. Officer in the military?

62456879
>>62456828
I have no idea because I've never been in the military and never wanted to.

62456897
>>62456891
Oooohhh that's two wrong in a row. Don't worry you still have one more shot at bringing the thread back on topic.

Help answer the topic of the thread by the gracious OP:
>I joined the military and I wanna know how to get a million dollars. I put 10% of my income into my retirement account and the rest of my free cash into index funds.

Grand prize is a free night stay at the Par-A-Dice Hotel and Casino! You wouldn't want to fuck this one up, Robert!

62456913
>>62456828
That question was answered forever ago before you faggots started your Robert script (note you were nowhere near the first ITT to call out the poster's retarded false claims about how VA works).

Keep investing in the TSP. The only thing I'd add is make sure he's putting it in the "C" Fund and not the retarded default "G" Fund.

Now will you fags stop hijacking OP's thread?

62456928
>>62456897
What's the topic?

62457039
>>62456528
Seeing as how they are gang stalking Robert for warning people about joining the military I would be interested in helping pay for a lawyer if I knew more about him. Maybe ask ACUL if they want to peruse this case (unlikely). Heard from other whistle blowers they all regularly say this sort of stuff when the camera is off. It is the ones who either say it on camera or on the internet that get punished. Considering that jannies and paid shills regularly break their own rules on /k/ I believe it.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ABx4fG1ZJL4

VbMQveZu and Tgdq/+4p are glowie shills.

62457052
>>62457039
>gang stalking
Meds.

62457053
>>62457039
gang stalking isn't real. What I have are a couple of secret admirers who religiously read my twitter account and talk about me on 4chan all day.

62457076
>>62456736
Uh about that... Which brand of communism? East German with their discount Nazi uniforms? Figures since they were the most functional communists.

62457081
>>62456897
Tell him to wait for the bear market. 2030 sound good?

62457082
>>62457076
I'm a Maoist.

62457474
>>62447595
I'm a Veteran. 7 years. Officer Corps. I did not get rich but I saw a few of my peers get rich and this advice only works if you're an Officer (single Enlisted are stuck in the barracks. Sucks to suck).

1) Do not get married
2) Do not spend money on women
3) Choose an MOS that deploys FREQUENTLY (you are guaranteed to deploy more frequently than Special Forces if you go Air Defense in the Army).
3) Get a VA loan to buy a Quadplex at your first duty station.
4) VA loan can only be taken out to buy your primary residence but if you PCS, you now need a new primary residence. You've built 2-3 years of equity in the first property, which you now rent out through a property management company.

Bonus: Set up your own property management company and start managing other peoples properties, too.

5) Repeat Steps 3 and 4 until you get out of the military.

6) Go to the doctor for literally anything and everything. When you separate you will have so many service connected injuries that youll get 100% VA Disability rating ($4k tax free each month) and with that rating, free medical coverage through the VA (a massive savings in medical costs)

You can also abuse the infinite certification and education programs they offer (and you should). They say not to leave the military with less education than you had going in. Take advantage of that. If you ever find yourself in a bullshit job where you're not in the field every 3 months, and you're able to take every 4 day weekend off without hassle, use that time to build a business of some sort.

Anyone who joins the military with a plan of action in place can really turn their life around. Maybe not into millionaire status, but into a comfortable, never NEED to work again type of life.

62457579
>>62447595
the artstyle and color scheme on vanilla was so good its insane. we hit diminishing returns on polygons a long time ago

62458199
>>62457474
>2
Hookers are a military man's best friend. New Cars and divorces destroy so many finances.

Medical thing is critical, I had that taught to me and too many leave it on the table..

Deployment has to be something gay and retarded, nothing lethal

Business is hard to get into but basic money management with a budget sheet tracking your expenses are critical. The amount of shiny impulse buys add up and are easily forgotten about, hence a budget sheet

t. Military with a bs job

62458277
>>62456428
You could do the same with Manhattan too for $5,073 /mo. 36 months, not too bad. Get a hybrid course. I asked my coworker after posting this and he says he's down to going in person once a quarter

62458320
>>62457474
Wow what a fucking scam the military is

62459412
>>62458320
Fraid so

62460073
>>62458320
>>62459412
What exactly is the scam here?
Getting money and bennies for your work?
Civilian sector fags are so fucking cucked they don't get PTO, pensions, bonuses, raises, or any job security.
Not even mentioning the tax-free allowances, free travel, waived fees, 0% down home loan, 5% match on 401k, free money falling from the sky, etc.
I can only assume you guys are assmad jealous because the corporations you work for (or wish to work for) don't compensate you like this.

62460086
>>62460073
>>work
slave morality

62460104
>>62460086
I'm sure being a doordasher is much better: >>62455670

62460157
>>62460073
I'm a veteran

62460175
>>62457081
It stops at 2059. It's not a coincidence.

62460188
>>62457076
Not him. Not a bolshevik.
But USSR = Union of Soviet Socialists Republic.
Socialism =/= communism.
Even they didn't have the balls to call themselves communists.
But retarded americans always thought they were.

item.title: Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL… — 75 replies — 300.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL…

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-07-08 15:22 UTC

OP

Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
>Even if you're "beating inflation" with a mortgage you're paying much more than its actual cost
>Closing costs
>All just so you can sell it (likely at a loss when considering inflation) so you can buy a slightly less expensive house
It's not that buying a house is a bad purchase if you can afford it, but acting like it's somehow an "investment" is absolutely retarded, you'd probably make more back from buying Pokemon cards

Early replies (first 3)

62450381
>>62450377
You're supposed to buy houses on a discount
>t. Selling for double what I purchased in 2023 after $20K in upgrades LOL

62450391
>>62450377
Idk I just want one to live in, but maybe they rent it that way person who rents pays bills and when he break something you can tell him to pay for it and then when they don't give a fuck anymore they sell it

62450397
>cbf moving every year because the landlord decides they a wankers and want to sell to some Chinese immigrants who took our jobs

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62457179
>>62457177
these threads are just made by either a bot or some discord tranny. We can the same one every few days. Don't reply in good faith

62457226
>>62457177
the top 0.000000001% do not own residential properties.

62457260
>>62457179
It's like doing the daily crossword. I come on 4chan and look for retarded ideas. They're easy to find here, almost the entire site is retardation. Then I try to identify the exact problems with the reasoning behind the ideas, and find a succinct way to disprove it. I'll usually post this disproof because it drives 4chan nuts when people disagree with their stupidity. They spout more nonsense in defense of their earlier nonsense and I get to analyze and diagnose that shit as well. Perhaps respond to it if it's particularly stupid.

it's a hobby. Keeps my critical thinking skills sharp and reminds me that most humans are dumber than children.

62457295
>>62457260
but anon, they're being retarded on purpose. You're losing

62457303
>>62457295
their motives have nothing to do with my hobby
and from what I've seen almost nobody here is pretending to be retarded. They might think they're pretending, but they're not.

>You're losing
there's nothing to win or lose

62458442
>>62450446
>It’s hard to lose money from owning a house.
Nigga what? You lose opportunity cost of down payment, you lose maintenance, you lose insurance, you lose interest payments, you lose opportunity cost of moving for better jobs. Houses you live in are never an asset.

62458496
>>62450377
Because its good for
>Banks
>Financial advisors
>Accountants
>Lawyers
>Real Estate agents
>Construction industry
>Furniture industry
But if you stop and actually think about it for yourself, its fucking stupid. Rent and actual assets is way more liberating and wealth creating.

62458570
>>62450377
Mortgages are the most accessible form of leverage known to and accepted by people. Here at least you can have zero downpayment, borrow 500k and 'gamble' it into RE. If it pays off, that is fucking sweet.

62458626
>>62458496
Rentoid cope

62458923
>>62458626
Housecuck rage because your suburbian house in ohio is depreciating while the equityGOD's portfolio is outpacing your wealth by 3 times

62458933
>>62450377
>Property taxes
Rentoids still pay them, just indirectly

62459040
>>62457226
You mean Elon musk?

62459092
>>62450377
That's the thing, it's not ment to be an investment, but every boomer and their accountant said:
"Buy an investment property, passive income, rent comes in regularly, sell it when you retire for fun money"
Then every government fed into this so they didn't have to pay social security, and now we've ended up here.

62459180
>>62450381
>t. Selling for double what I purchased in 2023 after $20K in upgrades LOL
you left out the part where you're lateraling into another plywood box that also doubled in price since 2023 with $20k of upgrades.

62459189
>>62456628
What does this even mean? Lol

62459200
>>62459180
He can't even afford that, he's priced out of the shitboxes he flipped and is moving back in with mommy and daddy. Just like the other rentoids he's basically hoping for a crash.

62459207
>>62450377
You're supposed to live your life you fucking retard
Marry and reproduce

62459227
>>62458442
>you lose maintenance
How much does that cost?
>you lose insurance
Optional if you own the home. Even then, I paid $1700 last year for insurance which is nothing. That’s easily 1 months rent by itself.
>job opportunities
Non issue
>houses you live in are never an asset
I can sell my house for more than I paid. You can’t sell the apartment that you dumped 5-6 figures into over the years. The choice is obvious

62459229
>>62459207
marriage and children is the death of the individual.

62459231
>>62459229

And ?

62459234
>>62459229
Ew fuck off you selfish retard

62459240
>>62459229
Reddit moment. You gonna quote Nietzsche next?

62459243
>>62459234
>>62459240
shouldnt you be getting your wife's son ready for the day?

62459270
>>62459262
Based

62459719
>>62459227
>How much does that cost?
Typically 1-3% of the home value annually is the expectation on average but it seems more spaced out than that. I.e. roof goes bad 16k bill one year, next year maybe minimal expense basic maintenance items like filters, or an AC servicing for a few hundred.

>Optional if you own the home. Even then, I paid $1700 last year for insurance which is nothing. That’s easily 1 months rent by itself.
If you think home insurance is optional then lmao, I would never have a 300k+ possession that could be wiped out by some other idiots stupidity or natural disaster but you do you. Where I love its not too bad but certain states or areas have very high insurance costs (FL, CO, CA, etc.) Worth considering.

>Non issue
What if you get laid off? What if you get a job offer for 60% higher salary but it requires you to move 3 hours away?

>I can sell my house for more than I paid. You can’t sell the apartment that you dumped 5-6 figures into over the years. The choice is obvious
I can sell my shares of VOO too. It depends on where you live and if you inflation adjust property values. For example, where i live property has increased by 3.7% since 2023, that sounds pretty good until you realize that cumulative inflation was 9.84% over the same duration meaning you lost money relative to inflation in my region as a home owner. You also don't get to sell the interest the bank made off you during ownership, that piece of your mortgage payment (around 80%-85% for the first 5 years) is exactly like rent, poof. You also dont get back the real-estate transaction fees, realtor fee, and transfer taxes that is usually around 8-15k for a 300k house just gone. Homeowners also dump 5-6 figures into keeping their homes just in taxes usually. Let's be honest, owning a home is a nice thing but it costs you money and is not an asset, it is a necessity that you live somewhere but there are more efficient ways to use your capital if you want to be actually wealthy.

62460098
>>62459719
>$16k for a new roof
Who the fuck is paying $16k to have their roof reshingled? I paid $4500 years ago and I only did that because the old shingles were at least 20 years old and discolored. Other “maintenance and repair” expenses are minimal in the grand scheme of things.

>If you think home insurance is optional then lmao
It is… There is no law demanding a home owner, who owns a house that’s paid off (which mine is), to have insurance. The only reason I have it is because paying ~$1700ish a year is pocket change. I live in FL btw.

>what if…..
I’m not even going to bother. We can spend all day going back and forth playing this game.

62460105
>>62460098
I see anon prefers roofs with 5 year lifespans instead of 20.

62460136
>>62455984
I was going to say the same, got mine at 362k with 3.25% and I'm sitting on a gold mine. House already gained 150,000 in equity in 6 years.

62460152
>>62450377
It lets me fuck women behind door which is quite a good investment for my cock and balls and gives me the reasurement I'm not giving my money to some boomer faggot in exchange of nothing which is again a good investment for my soul.

62460218
Its a good investment if you rent it out and can make back what you spent on it, it becomes free-ish passive income, that said being a good landlord is a skill and shit ones cant break even, you are not some globohomo LLC who can take a total loss if you let in some nigger tenants

Its also a good investment if you are renting, because renting is literally throwing money into the trash, whore yourself our or sell crack but for the love of god find a way to get enough for a downpayment, every month of rent is a month of your life wasted

if you already have something or manipulated family members to stay with them rent free, real estate is a shit investment

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 178 replies — 149.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 178 • Updated: 2026-07-08 14:14 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62456862
>>62456559
Let's just say that we won.

62456877
>>62437938
WAGMI

62457059
BATHROOM GODS
GET FUCKING READY
THIS IS THE ENDGAME
CONGRATULATIONS TO ALL THAT MADE IT THIS FAR
ENJOY!!!!!!!!!

62457184
Will this also benefit GME?
The other thread has too many shills to get an honest answer.

62457192
>>62457184
pools closed, this is a gated community and its also whites only.
head back

62457193
>>62457184
ultimately it's not going to benefit BBBY losers or GME owners.

it's meaningless noise signifying nothing except that the BBBY BK is winding ever closer to its finish without shareholders getting a dime.

62457200
>>62457193
that's because the bathroom gods will be getting way more than a dime

62457215
>>62457200
He asked for an honest answer and I gave it

you are getting nothing

if you get something from a class action lawsuit (unlikely) it's probably going to be less than a hundred dollars total. But in reality there's a long line of actual creditors and lawyers in front of you that will take any payments that might be won.

62457221
>>62457215
we won though

62457223
>>62457221
>we won though
retard of the year award perhaps.

62457418
>>62457215
less than a hundred per share aint bad tho.

I wonder what RC & Icahn are up too?

62457938
>>62449186
What is the legal process to uncancel a stock and reopen short positions that were closed upon cancelation? I can't seem to find it in current US securities law? AI can't find it for me either and is also unable to find me any cases of this ever happening. Looks to me like this is not possible under current law.

62457968
>>62457938
It's the exact same as if you were naked short a stock that issues a dividend. The company doesn't issue more money, they shorts simply are on the hook to pay for it.
Naked shorting creates synthetic longs. Disbursements to the shareholders by the estate/corporate entity are calculated by the actual TSO. It is not the prerogative of the company to "honor" counterfeits. The shorts pay for it.

62458028
>>62457938
what your looking for is the fact that the chapter 11 plan can still legally change, and equity/cash can be distributed to previous shareholders.

then what this poster said:
>>62457968

Short positions aren't reopened because they were never closed since the company is still in active chapter 11, and when the company give $ and shares to each previous shareholder they only have to make distributions based on the number of shares issued, not the extra billions that were created by market makers as Bed Bath pushed under 10 cents.

Nice try paid stock basher, but nothing you say can argue with reason.

62458266
>>62457938
There is no plan to “uncancel” the stock. That is a strawman argument you are forced to beat into the ground.

62458308
>>62457184
GAMEshire BATHaway
>t. pulte
>t. sultan

62458310
>>62458028
>Nice try paid stock basher
I think this whole thing must be some elaborate comedy bit, or you people are just insane. Good luck though.

62458385
>>62457184
>>62458308
It's as shrimple as that

62458481
>>62458308
>>62458385
Will this lead to MOASS?

62458756
>>62458481
We will finally be free

62458765
>>62458756
Don't say stuff that makes me feel sorry for you

62459593
>>62458765
Nothing really to feel sorry for other than some lost time. We already know the shareholder interests have been preserved because we know the CUSIP is still being used. We're just waiting to see what truly took place and from there we'll get our massive tendy payout.

62459685
Imagine missing out on the single greatest victory for retail investors in the entire history of finance. Not me because I am a bathroom god.

62459707
>>62459685
It really hasn't set in yet that we're getting extremely close to the finish line here. Normally I am kind of sad about the summer days passing by. But this fall is going to be spectacular.

62459753
>>62459707
Tendie status: in the fryer
Butterfly status: emerging from the cocoon
Shill status: in shambles

62459815
>>62459753
It's all coming together nicely. And it's not even just a small little payout either. We have billionaires calling this thing Gameshire Bathaway.
It's pretty awesome being on the inside of this joke being made. That goes for all of us, Maxipads included.

62459836
>we call GME maxi pads because they are all being used to soak up the fertilizer from $GABBBYQ's aunt flo reverse swaps at the holiday inn

62459881
0€ in my account now.
From 10.000€ in 2023 and 10k share BBBYQ.

62459892
>>62458756

62459901
I'm m very disappointed.
Europoor meanwhile.

item.title: AI is dying — 32 replies — 128.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI is dying

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-07-08 15:18 UTC

OP

>Over the weekend, I got a message from a tech company CEO outlining his TOTAL BAN ON AI across his organization. There are no exclusions. It is not limited to generative AI. It is not temporary. It is a full stop.
>It turns out that the corporate dream of a single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

Sorry not sorry, you've been warned multiple times but kept throwing money into casino chips aka AI stocks.

https://www.inc.com/joe-procopio/the-first-company-wide-ai-ban-just-hit-my-inbox-heres-what-it-means/91357535

Early replies (first 3)

62449971
>The Ban on AI Research
>The CEO’s company had been dealing with a slump in conversion rates for the better part of 2026
>Turns out, AI-assisted research had resulted in casting a much wider net for prospects and dragged in many that were not nearly a fit. In some cases, the companies were defunct, and in one case, the company didn’t exist at all. The best the CEO could figure is that AI had just inferred it into existence

62449974
>The Ban on AI Agents
>One of the agents broke, in that was unable to scrape data it needed to generate a report. Its solution was to just generate the report anyway and then to freak out about the sudden change in what that report was saying, now that it was missing foundational information but treating it as if that entire business line had suddenly gone to zero.
>“It took a week to figure it out, because the guy who created the agent wasn’t the guy responsible for the report, and he was stalling while re-running months’ worth of reports by hand.”

62449977
>The Ban on Vibe Coding
>The reporting failure was the trigger for the ban.
>“When I discovered we had two different versions of the same module in our platform, connected to two different databases filled with two different sets of data stored in two completely different and unrelated [schemas], the next day I put the ban in place.”

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449981
>>62449963
I'm happy for you, or sorry that happened to you.

62449983
>>62449963
>believing corporate media slop intended to make poorfags stay out

62450012
>>62449983
>they don't want us to make money

62450055
>>62449963
>single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

It is, but there is so little "100x" human and definitely no one is working for that specific company.

62450135
>>62450012
Speaking for myself, I do not like the idea of poors entering my country club.

62450162
>>62449963
It isn't dying, but people are waking up to the fact that it's not "Jarvis, run my company" situation and that there is a hard skill curve to coordinating agent pipelines.

62450194
>>62450012
True, but the game is already rigged for the casino either way.

62450337
Not reading all that but AI isn't going anywhere, the pace may slow down but it's not going to be binned off.

62450351
CEOniggers once again proven to be 40 IQ retards.

62450414
>>62449963
Our CEO is going all-in on AI for software development and it has boosted our throughput by 2-3x. It's not slop code, either.
We have unlimited tokens.
t. SWE at startup

62450423
>>62449963
Over the weekend my ceo told me we are adopting more ai

62452074
>>62450414
>It's not slop code
lol. LMAO even

62452087
>inc.com
What
>a tech ceo
Which company

62452090
>>62450012
>rich people investing 3 trillion into AI the last 4 years
>Nvidia a 5 trillion dollar company
>the AI market is over now, because it's over
Makes you think.

62452160
>>62450135
Exactly. There is the door so would you kindly leave?

62452209
Kek my company banned it years ago when some high level executive fed sensitive financial data to chat gpt. It qas back when chat gpt was new and novel and he was probably a boomer that didn't know what chatbots are and thought it was a super intelligence.

62452880
AI is a technology and it won't go way, it will evolve into different tools that will enhance the production. The problem is that corporations are using as an excuse to control the resources and make big data centers to get more info from you and sell it.

62452896
>>62449963
This article is a month old, fucktard.

62452909
>>62452896
Precisely, baggie.

62452925
>>62452909
continuation pattern

62452935
>>62452925
lmao

62452942
>>62452935
>you: heh my pattern is bearish
>"i disagree, I think it's bullish"
>REEEEEEEE TA SUCKS FUCK YOU

62453140
>>62452942
I never implied that. I implied you're delusional if you believe exponential growth is any sustainable long-term.

62453190
>>62453140
Probably because you are retarded.

62454009
>>62452074
All the code is written and reviewed by hand. We have custom AI agents for repetitive tasks and custom tool calls error-checking.
Works great, amazing ROI

62454042
>>62449963

There is no way I am going to ever write a regular expression or a complex SQL statement without AI EVER AGAIN.
AI solution saves time and is easy to verify.

62455423
>>62449963
its joever

62455925
>>62454009
We don’t even review our AI code anymore. Our CTO admitted the pace at which we are vibe coding quality code and feature delivery far outweighs the cons and our SWEs can keep up with helping us troubleshoot but can’t keep up with actually reviewing the code. We launched a new product and between me and one other person built the whole thing over 2,000 PRs from scratch in 3 weeks. Currently customers on it generating 6 figures in new revenue monthly, almost zero issues and when they do happen 98% of the time I just take the error throw it at Claude and it’s fixed 10m later. Crazy. I have zero coding experience btw, though I was a product manager for many years so led multiple teams of SWEs and have a clear advantage over most non coders in using AI for that and many other reasons. I keep thinking I should sell my house because I realize I’m pretty early to understanding how truly powerful this shit is and I think a lot of people are going to lose their jobs within the next few years, though I hope I’m wrong.

62460197
>>62455925
source: your ass

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 119 replies — 103.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 119 • Updated: 2026-07-08 14:16 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

62454511
>>62426638
this but unironically

62454976
I sold at 115k so no more plans for me.

62455432
>>62454976
Ok

62455827
>>62426535
How you doing now, bobo?

62459212
>>62455827
Lmao

62459912
>>62444587
I like the idea behind their TBV, as it helps to keep all transactions on the BTC chain

item.title: I've been all-in LINK since March 2019 (pre Google Cloud partnership). — 14 replies — 55.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been all-in LINK since March 2019 (pre Google Cloud partnership).

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-08 15:10 UTC

OP

I've been all-in LINK since March 2019 (pre Google Cloud partnership). I'm so sick, depressed and demoralized by the price action, I can't believe every single positive news crashes the price of Chainlink. This feels like some sick and twisted joke. I have 5800 tokens and thats barely 40k after 7 years of holding. If somebody told me back then that Chainlink would have traded at single digits in 2026 I would have brushed him off as a retard. I can barely get out of bed, I lost all my motivation and will to do anything. On top of that I have two very bad dental decays that will cost me thousands of dollars. My life has been ruined by this token. How many more years do we have to wait?

Early replies (first 3)

62458655
Just two more years bro.

62458665
>>62458650
>>62458655
>"hello fellow linkies I am so depressed we should all think of selling :("
>followed by samefagging 2 mins in, to be followed by more as per usual
You have to resort to this kind of thing because you're an impotent brown faggot who couldn't even keep the pool empty
Herbs in options

62458670
>>62458665
are you happy about the price action? Are you some kind of masochist that enjoys it? Because I'm certainly not and I'm tired of the gaslighting

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62458741
>>62458670
No but complaining about it wont do much

Either keep holding or sell your stack

62458750
well bud you got two possible futures to look forward to. maybe the clarity act scam will pass and maybe that will finally pump your stinky bags. or, if it doesn't pass, maybe you can look forward to a newly empowered and resurgent sec going scorched earth all of these scamming cryptoshits

62459960
>>62458741
I'm not even complaining, I'm just pissed

62460018
>>62458650
>I can't believe every single positive news crashes the price of Chainlink.
You mean "crashes the price of btc"

62460027
>>62459960
>I'm not even complaining
>I can barely get out of bed
>I lost all my motivation and will to do anything
>I have two very bad dental decays that will cost me thousands of dollars
>My life has been ruined by this token
what an absolute faggot you are, kill yourself

62460031
>>62458650
Don't be depressed now, be depressed later when it goes lower, back to $5!

62460034
>>62458665
Dude I'm white and I sold all my link. Link is a brown coin

62460077
>>62460027
okay maybe I'm complaining but I have a right to do so, nobody fucking told me that Chainlink was a 15 year hold. Everyone expected triple digits back in 2021 and here we are. I'm basically broke and my mouth fucking stinks, need to get an appointment asap. Fuck the gaslighters and fuck you too

62460088
For me, I could've retired in 2021 with Link but chose to wait for $70 which I thought was a fair price to hold for.

Now, I am so far off retirement that it does make me regret things enormously.

One thing I would say, is that debauchery in retirement may have broken me up with my girlfriend, so at least we're still together

62460141
hey look on the brightside, sergey is now a billionaire off of your stupidity, and he's STILL eating big macs every day and not giving a fuck! LOL!

62460154
>>62460077
>Everyone expected triple digits back in 2021
And that's exactly what would've happened if Bitcoin hadn't dumped on every single bit of Link news.

item.title: Is it true that we can cure the affordability crisis and inflation if we all just buy stonks? — 17 replies — 55.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it true that we can cure the affordability crisis and inflation if we all just buy stonks?

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-07-08 15:07 UTC

OP

Is it true that we can cure the affordability crisis and inflation if we all just buy stonks?

>OK, final thought. Um, record high in the stock market. You may know that. Oil prices coming down. That was easily predictable. And they’ll continue to drop. We hope they go below $70 a barrel.

>Now, the American oil companies will take their sweet time because they want to make as much money as possible, from us, dropping at the pump, but it’ll happen. But the most important thing is that, about 40% of Americans have no stocks, no mutual funds, no IRA, nothing.

>Okay. 58% according to Gallup have stocks. This is how you grow your money in a capitalist society.
Now if you don’t have any stocks, what deduce do you think is going to happen to you when you get older? What do you think that the government is going to provide a comfortable living for you? Is that what you think?

>You’ll get Social Security, but that eeks by. You want to live in a trailer?

>Stock market is the way to amass capital. And I’m not a speculator. I just tell my guys, I have a financial advisor and you know, you have to have one by the S&P, the Standard & Poors. That means that by and just hold it. My portfolio is doing great because Trump, his policies, appeal to the big companies, but 40% of Americans don’t participate in capitalism and a lot of them are yowling about high prices?
Come on. You get 4% inflation here. Live in the real world. So you younger people, you got to get into the stock market. Don’t take big risks. Go with the conservative stuff. Final thought.

Early replies (first 3)

62459793
Its an income problem. People arent even buying fastfood as much anymore.

62459803
>>62459782
The guy you're quoting is a nepobaby coke addict.

62459807
Is it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62459832
>>62459782
>Buy my bags saar!
No

62459839
>>62459793
>People arent even buying fastfood as much anymore.

Lmao, maybe the losers learned to cook

62459866
>>62459793
I am surprised restaurants aren't mega dumping. They're getting hit with people having less income/income not going as far and GLP-1s becoming more and more accessible.

62459872
>>62459866
Theyre all buying Sysco slop and frozen shit to cut costs.

62459879
>>62459866
Have you looked at the stocks moron

62459926
>>62459782
The cure to the affordability crisis is mass production of physical goods and services. I've already gone over this, but I have zero power and nobody gives a shit anyways. You can't tax your way out of this. You can't loan/print your way out of this. You can't "muh labor unions & eat the rich" your way out of this. And you can't "Luigi" your way out of this.

You also can't regulate your way out of this. Regulations are a major cause for all of this in the first place. They make it nearly impossible to build power plants or utility stations, they make it nearly impossible to build housing anywhere, they make it nearly impossible to experiment with new drugs, they make it nearly impossible to build new cars and they give "free" money to 18 year old tards that don't understand budgeting, while letting university administrators (95% Democrat) hike tuition fees to infinity. This is why everyone is either creating software apps on phones, crypto, or other tech related shit, because it's one of the least regulated industries.

You need mass production, which means:
- Eliminate 90% of current regulations in every industry.
- Eliminate all required hiring practices and have complete freedom of association.
- Eliminate 90% of taxes/tariffs and allow both corporations and consumers to decide where their money flows. The only things we should be funding are military, courts and direct consumer subsidies to those with actually physical and mental health handicaps, not the scams we have today.
- Serious crackdown on activist judges and radically harsher penalties for criminals.
- Privatize Social Security and turn it into a forced private savings program, like a 401k, that's only accessible at age 65+. People should control their retirement, not the government.

62459931
>>62459782
>>62459926
- Privatize Medicare & Medicaid and turn them effectively into forced HSA accounts. Allow consumers to choose any policy, from any company, at any time. End subsidies to corporations and force them to compete for their customers. Anybody that is truly impoverished and cannot fund an HSA should get a direct subsidy from the government into their account. This way they still get to choose their insurance plan and the insurance providers still need to compete for their business.
- Deport all illegal aliens.

62459940
Bill o really with some solid advice. Too bad im buying massive puts against the market

62460007
>>62459879
No.

62460013
>>62459782
>Get in the stock market
>Trump launches Iran War part 2.0
>Lose money anyways
I hate you all..

62460061
>>62460013
I boated oil today usa out of vc 2 days ago was the sign of the jew, man jews are ez nowadays.

62460067
>>62460013
Be thankful the markets already shit or youd have really gotten fucked

62460107
>>62459926
>>62459931
>Everyone and everything just needs less regulation and more privatization
How, in your mind, does that solve anything? How would privatizing social security make it better?

>you need mass production
Of what? What can we make that isn't already meeting the demand for it?
"Just make shit"? What? Tables? Hula hoops?

62460134
>>62460107
Put what I posted into Chat-GPT and tell it to steelman my argument. Ask it more questions if you're interested. AI does a fairly good job steelmanning arguments if you specifically tell it to.

>you need mass production
>Of what? What can we make that isn't already meeting the demand for it?
Housing, healthcare, energy, vehicles, literally everything people online complain about on a daily basis.

item.title: Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed. — 34 replies — 51.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-07-08 14:46 UTC

OP

Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Now many crypto's like XRP and Chainlink are no longer illegal and the banks can start buying it.

Are you ready for the pump of a lifetime in the coming weeks?

Early replies (first 3)

62444894
I just took 70mg of illicit amphetamine sulfate

62444895
>>62444894
That's very nice to hear, I hope you are enjoying yourself.

62444904
>>62444895
it's nothing like meth (oral ROA meth had no euphoria either), minimal vasoconstriction; none really. not euphoric like adderall either, it feels like super non-diuretic caffeine. this has a 1:1 dextro:racemic ratio; so the pharma equivalent is evekeo.

I thought it was bunk but my heart rate is elevated but no arrhythmia like meth (that shit is poison, it'll keep you up for 3-5 days with no perceptual fatigue at significant cost)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62444911
>>62444904
>>62444906
okay well thats nice and all but not really what my thread was about

62444922
>>62444911
hm, do you know how it impacts privacy coins like XMR or even zioncash (ZEC)?

62444929
>>62444922
i do not

62444934
>>62444929
alright, I appreciate it regardless. thoughts on CBDCs?

62444939
>>62444934

I actually dont know much about that also

62444940
>>62444939
you sound like a nice guy no homo

62444948
these 2 got married y'know

62444959
>>62444940
Haha thanks man you too

>>62444948
Nooo we are just friends haha

62444978
>>62444892
> CLARITY ACT
wasn't there some announcement announced for yesterday?

62444991
op they haven't voted on it and aren't close to voting on it. they're hoping to get it done before august 10

62444995
>>62444991
Somone told me clarity act was 4th of july a few weeks ago

62445015
>>62444948
naw, the no homo made marriage impossible
>>62444959
>we are just friends
this

62445147
>>62444978
No, Trump did an interview restating that the US will be the crypto capital of the world.
>>62444892
CLARITY will pass, but there is a lot going behind the scenes that's causing it to stall. Did you know that CLARITY allows banks to buy and sell crypto? The last time US banks traded stocks was before the great depression.

62445160
>>62445147
Oh that's very interesting, thanks for sharing my friend

62445194
So, two more weeks?

62445212
>>62444948
you're responding to a samefag trying to keep his thread at the top because no one cares.

>>62444892
no one cares

62445217
>>62445212
that's not me

62445568
Why isnt crypto pumping harder?

62448360
>>62444892
The banks can already buy both. ETF inflows broke records despite being from small issuers on XRP, highest of any asset on the year, while inflows are borderline non-existent for link.

62448734
Everyone itt thread is a retarded bot.
Fake thread.

62450765
>>62444892
BOOOOOOOOOM

62450968
I hate niggers

62451473
>>62450968
Kaboom

62454381
>>62444892
>banks start buying it
okay jeet

62454867
>XRP

So. Fucking. Bullish.

62458344
>>62444892
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62458384
>>62444892
>this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.
nope

62458627
>>62444995
that date was always nonsense because they are literally in recess from the end of june until july 13. they will have another month to pass it before they go into recess yet again in august

62459378
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62460039
>>62444978
2 more weeks turned into 1 more month

item.title: A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn… — 14 replies — 15.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-08 14:31 UTC

OP

A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

Early replies (first 3)

62448322
>>62448316
Just bullshit. At 4% annually your link would need to be worth $9125 per token.

62448378
>>62448316
He is just talking bullshit. They just pushed all their users to pay through stripe. There is not really a pathway to imagine corporate successes translating into token holder value. Payment abstraction layer and staking were both a flop and they were what link holders always said would be the way they would make it but now instead they deleted the past marketing materials, disallowed comment on it in their AMA and pushed Stripe payments. It has unironically never been more obvious and is so obvious it is borderline in the exit phase

62448411
>>62448322
10k Link eoy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448505
I wish.

62448514
>>62448322
>>62448378
Kek why do fuddies always flag themselves as newfags by replying to pasta. Retarded fuddies. Just bought more LINK, seethe and dilate.

>posted from my iPhone from my beach house in Mexico

62448520
>>62448514
Not fudding - I bought another 1000 this week. What's your vision for where this is going?

62448521
>>62448316
if you follow the money there's a good reward waiting for those who stake

62450512
bump

62451573
>>62448520
I'm a cuckold and I just LOVE holding Chainlink. Whenever Sergey dumps millions of tokens on Binance I get super hard! Fuddies can cope and seethe but this is my fetish and you can't kink shame me

62451657
he didnt pay his ladyboy

62452033
>>62448514
Been here longer than you. I bought link in 2018 with early biz and sold when it pumped and it was clearly not building out for token holders alongside literally all of early biz before the necrotic, tech illiterate, petrified fossil of late normie greed cultists flooded in to fill the cavity and be the findom submissive of Sir Gay who sells to them with zero prospect of value return for token holders, even selling node operator rewards with the network unable to support 30% of function through revenues. You are not a smart investor, you literally just get hyped about cheap "announcements" which mean nothing for token holders whatsoever and are literally written to seem like they do with zero actual technical reality so that they can use them to sell tokens to you to fundraise for the company and salaries. Your analysis amounts to "price went up here before and I missed it but I won't miss THIS time because it will TOTALLY happen again, just..cuz!! Oh, did you see the news on x institution using CLL for price feeds?! I am going to be SO rich! No, I don't understand how this will impact actual token demand or how the token will fit into the equation at all, but the announcement is from Chainlink Labs, so it must somehow!!!"

62454188
>>62451573
The funny thing is this is LITERALLY the link holder psychology.

62455614
>>62448316
yes many rupees await you if you buy the Chainlink token sir

62459980
>>62448316
>$7
stinky linkies lost the game of life

62457961
>>62457516
Nice of you to calm down your spam posting after the ban you got from my reporting

62458335
hatin stinks
it's what we do
we'll never ever stop oh nooo

62458398
>>62457781
Wait, is this real?

62458399
>>62457534
>>62457837
>>62457961
The OP admitted to being lebanese early on, hence why they used the hijab avatar originally. Just another piece of third world garbage that sits on biz all day spamming fud

62458405

62458413

item.title: VOO and Chill — 194 replies — 45.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 194 • Updated: 2026-07-08 11:10 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62431922
>>62431920
Feel like VUG has consistently beaten VOO though

62431971
>>62431922
Contrarian.
I own VUG and don't own VOO tho :)

62432023
>>62431920
fair enough
thoughts on a younger person doing QQQM?

62434054
Anyone have thoughts or realized returns and experiences on VOO + satellite
accounts instead of just VOO

62435372
Was gonna try to go into this DRAM dip but decided to just do more VOO
But idk a higher return ETF still sounds promising and like a no brainer whether it’s SOXX or QQQM but QQQ has so much crossover with VOO it’s just redundant and only good for growth seasons
I guess this is why VOO and Chill exists to just stop thinking

62436303
>>62431920
What about QQQ though

62436357
i boughted my daily VOO
i'll keep dealing the real world like a good goyim in the mean time.

62436478
>>62436357
huzzah brother

62436499
>>62400493
Where my VGT bros at?

62436507
>>62400636
>Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....
You say this as if the average /biz/ shitcoin gambler is performing better than VOO which is definitely not the case.

62436510
>>62428031
Your IQ is so actually low. Holy shit.

62436538
>>62436510
Do you think DRAM has value, honest question, and for how long - if so, what dip should one wait for
>>62436499
VGT and QQQ are only doing better thanks to tech having such strong performances for years now, correct?
VOO seems safe since you don't know the future, but on the other hand, thinking the future of the market for the next decades wont always be tech focused seems so short sighted it's silly.....maybe I should start DCA into VGT?

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

62438775
Yep bros
Bought some more VOO and am waiting on my DRAM position to go green so I can sell and not have to deal with thematic ETFs no high expense ratios ever again
Simple as

62440169
>>62431374
>VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses
VOO is passively managed. These 2 girls are not like Kathy Wood picking stocks.

62442752
>>62431374
They could boil alive eat grannies alive for all I care as long as they get the money going, what kind of faggot ass train of thought is this. They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding. "The people making me rich are foids so it makes me icky" is unironically the foidest take I have ever read.
>>62430902
A year ago I started investing for the first time in my life (I'm in my mid 20s) and thought I was very smart and I would just outperform the market 1% profit at a time. After a whole year I only managed to get a net benefit of a 3% after wasting many hours and carrying heavy bags. I learnt my lesson. VOO and chill.

62442988
>>62442752
>They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding.

The portfolio managers of VOO have 0 control over whether VOO compounds or not. VOO is simply an ETF that tracks the S&P 500. Their only job is to make sure VOO follows the S&P 500. Once the fund is set up, there really is not much for them to do. I guess if they haven't automated removing and adding companies to the composition of VOO, then they do that.

62444521
Let’s all say thank you to Jack Bogle

62444531
>>62400515
and yet boomers hold 90% of the wealth and growing...

62445651
>>62421799
>5+ yr 60% drawdown event happens
>buy cheaper voo

62446571
>>62444521
thanks chad bogle

62447653
>>62445651
Kek it really is that simple

62450991
>>62446571
Thanks Chad Bogle!

62454214
>>62413718
Felix Prehn has never been an investment banker. He is an ex artist and wine salesman, now grifter on YouTube who makes money from his "academy" fees (up to 10k for shitty training). He's a regular scammer, like many in this segment.

62455460
It feels good to be a VOO’er

62455475
>>62400493
My retirement funds are $70k and in VOO, anything better to put all that in?

62455990
>>62455475
Look into VPMAX

62456048
>>62455990
Neat

62459187
>>62403053
The median 64 yo is planning to work until they're dead.
Boomers are never going to retire because they can't afford to. They are going to keep working like the good little goyim they are and fall down dead on the job because that's the only way they can fund the monthly payments on all the toys they went into debt to purchase.
I spit on their graves. Fuck boomer scum.

62459238
>>62459187
Lmao, another white incel who knows everything,,,,,what are the odds? 100% at least,,,,,do they use it to get rich? Lmao no
